topic: A Mongolian horseman using an Uurga to catch a wild horse, wide landscape shot.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Wide landscape front view of a lone Mongolian horseman with an Uurga, ready to lasso.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Side-angle as the horseman swings the Uurga to rope the wild horse.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: Close-up on the Uurga tightening around the wild horse, securing the catch.
parsed_generation.narration: An expansive steppe frame opens with the Mongolian horseman and his Uurga in a wide front view. It then follows a side-angle lasso swing toward the wild horse and finishes with a close-up of the Uurga securing the catch on the animal.

feedback: The concept stays on topic and uses a clear shot progression, but it deviates from the gold standard by using three shots instead of two and by not clearly depicting the high-speed gallop or the explicit lowering/tightening of the uurga loop. To align with the gold standard, structure the sequence as two 4-second segments: [00:00-00:04] a wide landscape of the steppe with a galloping rider holding the uurga, and [00:04-00:08] the rider extending/lowering the loop to capture the wild horse, with a close-up on the loop tightening.
strengths: Clear, concise shot-by-shot progression with distinct angles.
Action-first cinematic language that conveys the capture moment.
fixes: Adopt the two-shot, four-second-per-shot structure [00:00-00:04] and [00:04-00:08] to mirror the gold standard.
Emphasize high-speed gallop and the lowering/loop action of the uurga, including a close-up of the loop tightening.
